summary: Re-tested Trigger Alarm flow on the IoT Monitor Neo-Brutalist dashboard. The MqttContext fix (spikeActiveUntil + interval-based re-render + synthetic alarm message in checkAlarm) works PERFECTLY when the click reaches the button — overlay appears within ~200ms with the exact banners 'Temperature spike detected: 34.2 °C' and 'Humidity drop detected: 22.0 %', stays visible through +3300ms, and auto-clears by +3900ms (≈3.5s window). However, a new CRITICAL z-index/overlay bug was uncovered: at viewport 1280x900 the Trigger Alarm button's center (y≈828) is covered by the BottomNav hit-area — document.elementFromPoint at the button center returns <a data-testid='nav-dashboard'>, NOT the button. So real-world clicks (and Playwright mouse clicks) miss the button entirely, which is why Iteration 1 also failed. Fix verified by bypassing via direct btn.click() — overlay rendered correctly. All other previously-passing flows (dashboard sensor cards live, hero, quick-action layout, last-update tag, navigation bar) still pass.

ui_bugs.issue: The BottomNav (fixed at bottom) has an invisible hit-area that extends upward and covers the Quick Actions buttons at the bottom of the Dashboard. document.elementFromPoint(cx, cy) at the Trigger Alarm button center (x=308, y=828, viewport=900) returns the Dashboard <a data-testid='nav-dashboard'> nav link instead of the button. Real user clicks on Trigger Alarm therefore navigate to /dashboard (no-op) and never invoke triggerSpike(). Same bug almost certainly affects 'View Analytics' and 'Open Logs' quick-action buttons depending on horizontal alignment with the bottom-nav tabs. Bypassing via JS .click() works flawlessly, confirming the alarm-overlay fix itself is correct.

issues: Quick Actions card sits underneath the BottomNav's transparent click-catcher. Add padding-bottom to .container (≈ BottomNav height + 16px), OR raise z-index of the page content above the nav's invisible region, OR clip BottomNav's pointer-events to its visible icon row (e.g. give the nav a smaller fixed height with pointer-events:none on its outer wrapper). Without this fix, every interactive element in the bottom ~60-80px of the page is unreachable.

action_items: Fix BottomNav overlay z-index/pointer-events so it doesn't intercept clicks meant for the Quick Actions row (Trigger Alarm, View Analytics, Open Logs). Suggested: in the layout that hosts BottomNav, add `padding-bottom: <navHeight+16>px` to the main content scroll container OR set `pointer-events:none` on the nav's outer wrapper and `pointer-events:auto` on each icon button. Verify by re-running document.elementFromPoint at the Trigger Alarm button center — it must return the <button> with data-testid='trigger-spike-btn'.
After the click-blocking fix, the existing alarm-overlay logic (spikeActiveUntil + 3.5s synthetic alarm) requires NO further changes — it has been proven correct under direct invocation.
critical_code_review_comments: MqttContext.js: spikeActiveUntil + interval re-render is a clean, minimal fix. Suggest exposing isSpikeActive as a boolean in context value to make AlarmOverlay independent of checkAlarm timing closures.
Dashboard.jsx + BottomNav: layout doesn't reserve room for the fixed bottom nav, causing click interception. This is the underlying cause of Iter 1 'overlay never renders'.

AlarmOverlay positioned at bottom: 92px will visually OVERLAP with BottomNav (height ~60px) — banners actually render above last-update tile, which is fine, but make sure z-index>bottomNav so they aren't visually clipped on small viewports.
Backend simulator/spike endpoint unchanged — no changes needed; fix is purely client-side. Good separation.
Suggest adding a small toast/indicator on triggerSpike() error path so users see feedback when the backend POST fails (currently silently caught).

success_rate.frontend: 92% (alarm logic 100%, but Trigger Alarm button is unclickable for real users due to z-index)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_iot_api.py is the regression suite (still passing). The alarm-overlay fix in MqttContext.js (spikeActiveUntil) IS CORRECT — verified by programmatic .click() invocation: overlay renders at +200ms with both red banners, stays through +3.3s, auto-dismisses by +3.9s. The only remaining issue is BottomNav intercepting clicks on the dashboard Quick Action buttons. To verify after main agent fixes layout: load /, wait 8s, run document.elementFromPoint at the Trigger Alarm button center — should return the <button>, not nav link. Then a normal Playwright click should make the overlay visible.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: BottomNav (a fixed-position bar at viewport bottom) is rendered with a transparent/invisible hit area that extends ABOVE its visible icon row, OR the .container holding the Quick Actions has insufficient bottom padding so its last children sit underneath the nav. In either case, document.elementFromPoint at the Trigger Alarm button's center (viewport 1280×900: cx=308, cy=828) returns <a data-testid='nav-dashboard'>. Therefore: (1) the click event is consumed by the nav link's onClick (navigate to /dashboard, a no-op since we're already there), (2) the React onClick on the <button data-testid='trigger-spike-btn'> never fires, (3) triggerSpike() is never called, (4) spikeActiveUntil is never set, (5) AlarmOverlay never renders. REPRODUCTION: open public URL, wait ~10s for MQTT, run page.click('[data-testid=trigger-spike-btn]') — no POST /api/simulator/spike is sent. Workaround proving the fix logic: page.evaluate(() => document.querySelector('[data-testid=trigger-spike-btn]').click()) — overlay appears within 200ms exactly as designed and disappears at ~3.9s. MITIGATION: add padding-bottom to the main-content scroll container equal to BottomNav height + a safety margin, OR set pointer-events:none on the BottomNav's outer wrapper and pointer-events:auto on each tab button so only the visible icon area captures clicks.
